Mission: Impossible—Fallout (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $220,159,104Details
International Box Office $566,110,516
Worldwide Box Office $786,269,620
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,970,770
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $16,138,367
Total $23,109,137
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Ethan Hunt and his IMF team, along with some familiar allies are in a race against time after a mission gone wrong.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$61,236,534 (27.8% of total gross)
Legs:3.60 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:28.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$178,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,386 opening theaters/4,395 max. theaters, 6.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $289,034,342

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 27th, 2018 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
July 27th, 2018 (IMAX) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: July 16th, 2018 (Wide), released as Mission:Impossible- Fallout (South Korea)
July 25th, 2018 (Wide), released as Mission: Impossible Fallout (United Kingdom)
July 26th, 2018 (Wide), released as Misión: Imposible - Repercusión (Mexico)
July 26th, 2018 (Wide), released as Миссия невыполнима: Последствия (Russia (CIS))
July 26th, 2018 (Wide), released as Миссия невыполнима: Последствия (Turkey)
Video Release: December 4th, 2018 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for violence and intense sequences of action, and for brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2537 (Cert #51552), 7/25/2018)
Running Time: 147 minutes
Franchise: Mission: Impossible
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Intertitle, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: London, Paris
Plot point: C.I.A., Dream Sequence, False Identity, Hitmen, MI6, Nightmare, Non-Chronological, Prison Break, Revenge, Secret Agent, Surprise Twist
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Based on TV
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Skydance Productions, Bad Robot, Paramount Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
